chapter_context.summary: Josiah reads the Law, renews the covenant, and carries out a sweeping purge of idolatry across Israel and Judah; Josiah is slain at Megiddo by Pharaoh Necho; evil short reigns follow.

outline.text: Josiah Gathers All Judah; Reads the Book; Renews the Covenant
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Josiah Gathers All Judah; Reads the Book; Renews the Covenant (23:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Josiah's Comprehensive Reforms; Destroys High Places and Bethel's Altar
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Josiah's Comprehensive Reforms; Destroys High Places and Bethel's Altar (23:4-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.
This section focuses on Josiah Celebrates a Magnificent Passover (23:21-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Josiah Banishes Mediums; The Unrelenting Wrath of God Against Manasseh's Sins
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Josiah Banishes Mediums; The Unrelenting Wrath of God Against Manasseh's Sins (23:24-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Josiah Slain by Pharaoh Necho at Megiddo
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Josiah Slain by Pharaoh Necho at Megiddo (23:28-30), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jehoahaz's Evil Three-Month Reign; Deposed; Jehoiakim Placed on Throne
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehoahaz's Evil Three-Month Reign; Deposed; Jehoiakim Placed on Throne (23:31-35), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.
This section focuses on Jehoiakim's Wicked Reign Begins (23:36-37), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 23 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
